5-minute-baileys-chocolate-mousse.name: 5 Minute Baileys Chocolate Mousse
instructions: Combine heavy whipping cream, Bailey's, cocoa powder, powdered sugar, vanilla extract, and salt a large bowl. Beat with an electric mixer until stiff peaks form.
Serve immediately or chill until serving.

5-grain-porridge-with-bee-pollen-apples-.name: 5-grain Porridge With Bee Pollen, Apples, And Coconut

ingredients.item: Flaky sea salt (such as maldon) and bee pollen
Bee pollen is available at health food stores

instructions: Bring brown rice, quinoa, amaranth, millet, wheat bran, salt, and 6 cups water to a boil in a medium pot. Reduce heat, partially cover, and simmer, stirring occasionally, until cereal is the consistency of porridge (softer and thicker than the usual bowl of oatmeal) and water is absorbed, 40–50 minutes.
DO AHEAD: Porridge can be made 5 days ahead. Let cool; cover and chill.
Heat coconut oil in a medium skillet over medium-high. Cook coconut flakes, stirring often, until toasted, 2 minutes; transfer to a plate.
Add apple, cinnamon, and 2 Tbsp. honey to skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until apples are browned in spots and tender, about 3 minutes.
Serve porridge topped with apples, coconut, sea salt, bee pollen, and a drizzle of honey, if you like.

5-hour-beef-stew.name: 5-hour Beef Stew

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 121°C (120 degrees C).
Place beef into a roasting pan. Stir in potatoes, tomato-vegetable juice cocktail, carrots, celery, onion, tapioca, sugar, salt, and pepper. Cover the pan.
Bake in the preheated oven until beef and potatoes are tender, about 5 hours.

5-ingredient-chocolate-peanut-butter-cup.name: 5-ingredient Chocolate Peanut Butter Cups
instructions: Line a muffin tin with muffin tin liners.
In a bowl or measuring cup, add the dark chocolate and ¼ cup (50 g) coconut oil.
Stir in vanilla extract.
Microwave for 30-second intervals until melted, stirring each time.
Pour half of the chocolate mixture into the liners (just enough to cover the whole bottom). Save the other half for the top layer.
Freeze for 15 minutes.
In a bowl or measuring cup, add the peanut butter, honey, and 2 tablespoons coconut oil.
Microwave 15 seconds or until slightly melted and pourable.
Pour mixture evenly into the muffin tin.
Freeze for 5 minutes.
Pour remaining chocolate mixture on top of the peanut butter layer.
Freeze until firm about 1 hour.
Store in the refrigerator until ready to serve.
Enjoy!

5-ingredient-roasted-salmon.name: 5-ingredient Roasted Salmon
instructions: Preheat the oven to 218°C (220 degrees C). Line a rimmed baking sheet with foil and spray with nonstick spray.
Stir butter and molasses together in a small bowl.
Lay salmon, skin-side down, on the prepared baking sheet. Spread butter mixture evenly over the salmon. Sprinkle with Greek seasoning and drizzle with ½ of the lemon juice.
Roast in the preheated oven until salmon flakes easily with a fork, 17 to 20 minutes.
Sprinkle with remaining lemon juice and serve.

5-ingredient-tomato-sauce.name: 5-ingredient Tomato Sauce
instructions: Purée tomatoes in a food processor until they’re as smooth or chunky as you like. Transfer tomatoes to a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot. (Or, use an immersion blender and blend directly in pot.) Add garlic, oil, and a 5-finger pinch of salt. Bring to a boil and cook, stirring occasionally, until sauce is reduced by about one-third, about 20 minutes. Stir in butter.
Do Ahead: Sauce can be made 5 days ahead; cover and chill. Or let cool to room temperature and freeze up to 3 months.

5-ingredient-vegetarian-chili.name: 5-ingredient Vegetarian Chili
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add onions and sauté until transparent, 5 to 7 minutes.
Add tomatoes, beans, and chili powder; bring to a simmer. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes or longer.

5-layer-dip-wreath.name: 5-layer Dip Wreath
instructions: Place a glass upside down in the center of a springform pan.
Spread out desired amount of beans.
Spread out desired amount of cheese.
Spread out desired amount of pico de gallo.
Spread out desired amount of sour cream.
Spread out desired amount of guacamole.
Drizzle on lime juice, and sprinkle on chopped cilantro.
Place 3 cherry tomato halves in a cluster to look like holly berries and freeze for 15 minutes.
Remove springform pan and glass carefully. Serve with tortilla chips!
Enjoy!

5-minute-bulk-blender-pizza-sauce.name: 5-minute Bulk Blender Pizza Sauce
instructions: Blend tomato sauce, olive oil, sugar, thyme, basil, oregano, shallots, garlic powder, salt, and baking soda together in a blender on high until sauce is smooth and well-mixed, 1 to 2 minutes.
Divide sauce among twelve 1/2-cup freezer-safe containers; store in freezer until ready to use.
Remove individual containers from freezer to thaw before using, about 60 minutes.

5-minute-vegan-pesto.name: 5-minute Vegan Pesto
instructions: Combine basil leaves, olive oil, pine nuts, nutritional yeast, garlic, salt, and black pepper in the bowl of a food processor; pulse until smooth.

5-star-lemon-meringue-pie.name: 5-star Lemon Meringue Pie

ingredients.item: Deep dish graham cracker pie crust

instructions: Preheat oven to 218°C (220 degrees C).
Beat sweetened condensed milk, egg yolks, lemon juice, and lemon zest together in a bowl until smooth; pour into pie crust.

Beat egg whites with an electric mixer in a glass or metal bowl until foamy. Gradually add sugar and vanilla extract, continuing to beat until stiff peaks form. Lift your beater or whisk straight up: the egg whites will form sharp peaks; spoon onto the pie to cover completely.
Bake pie in preheated oven until the tips of the topping begin to brown, about 3 minutes.

50-clove-garlic-knots.name: 50 Clove Garlic Knots
instructions: Make the dough: In the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the hook attachment, add the water, sugar, 3 tablespoons olive oil, flour, yeast, and salt. Mix on low speed until the dough starts to come together. Increase speed to medium and knead for 7-10 minutes, until the dough is soft and elastic. The dough should spring back when touched.
Grease a clean large bowl with the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil. Add the dough, cover the bowl, and let rise in a warm place for about 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Meanwhile, preheat the oven to 191°C (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Divide the dough into 18 equal pieces. Working one at a time, roll each piece into an 8-inch log. Tie each log into a knot, tuck the 2 ends underneath, and place on the prepared baking sheet.
Use a pastry brush to lightly brush each knot with olive oil. Bake in the preheated oven for about 15 minutes, or until golden brown on top.

While the knots are baking, make the garlic butter: Melt the butter in a large high-sided skillet over medium heat. Add the garlic and cook for about 5 minutes, or until fragrant and softened. Stir often to prevent burning.
Transfer garlic butter to a large mixing bowl. Let cool slightly, then stir in the Parmesan cheese, parsley, and salt until combined.
Remove the knots from the oven. Let cool slightly, then transfer them to the garlic butter mixture. Toss until evenly coated and serve immediately.
Enjoy!

50-garlic-clove-potatoes-au-gratin.name: 50 Garlic Clove Potatoes Au Gratin
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200°C). Grease an 8x8-inch square baking dish with nonstick cooking spray.

Use a sharp knife to cut off the tops of the garlic heads and place them cut-side up on a large piece of foil. Drizzle the exposed garlic cloves with olive oil and season with salt. Bring up the corners of the foil around the garlic, then roll closed to form a tight packet. Place the packet on a baking sheet and roast in the preheated oven for about 45 minutes, or until the garlic is caramelized. Remove from the oven and let sit for about 15 minutes, or until cool enough to handle.

Unwrap the foil packet and squeeze the heads to release the roasted garlic cloves into an upright blender. Add the half-and-half, heavy cream, and cream cheese, followed by ½ cup Gruyère, ½ cup cheddar, 2 tablespoons Parmesan, and ½ teaspoon kosher salt. Blend until the sauce is smooth.
Arrange the sliced potatoes in even rows in the prepared baking dish.
Pour the sauce over the potatoes, making sure to get in between the potato slices.
Evenly top the potatoes with the remaining ½ cup Gruyère, ½ cup cheddar, and 2 tablespoons Parmesan. Season with thyme and freshly ground black pepper.
Cover the baking dish with foil and bake in the preheated oven for 40 minutes. Remove the foil and cook for another 15-20 minutes, until the potatoes are tender and the cheese is evenly browned.
Let cool slightly, then garnish with chives to serve.
Enjoy!

50-garlic-clove-shrimp-scampi.name: 50 Garlic Clove Shrimp Scampi
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rapid boil. Add the pasta and cook until al dente. Reserve about 1 cup of the pasta water then drain into a colander and set aside.
Pat the shrimp dry with paper towels and generously season with creole seasoning.
Heat olive oil in a large stick over medium heat. Add the shrimp and cook until about 1 to 2 minutes per side, or until they just begin to turn pink. Remove the shrimp and set aside.
Add butter to the skillet the shrimp was cooked in. Once the butter is melted, add the minced garlic and red pepper flakes. Cook until garlic is fragrant and softened, about 5 minutes.

Increase the heat to medium high then add in the wine and lemon juice. Use a spatula to scrape up any of the browned bits on the bottom. Continue to cook until the mixture is reduced by half, about 2 to 3 minutes.

Add the seared shrimp, cooked pasta and ½ cup reserved pasta water. Cook over high heat until everything is evenly combined and the sauce has thickened.  Add more pasta water as needed to achieve desired consistency.
Remove from heat and stir in the parsley and lemon zest. Serve immediately.
Enjoy!

50-clove-garlic-stuffed-focaccia.name: 50-clove Garlic Stuffed Focaccia

ingredients.prep: plus 2 tablespoons olive oil, divided, plus more as needed

instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200°C).

Roast the garlic: Using a sharp knife, slice the top ¼–½ inch off the tops of the garlic heads, exposing the individual cloves. Remove any loose, papery skins. Place the garlic cut-side up in the center of a large piece of foil.

Drizzle all the garlic heads with ¼ cup of olive oil and sprinkle with salt and pepper. Fold the foil around the garlic. Place in a small baking dish and roast in the oven for 40-60 minutes, or until the cloves are soft and creamy.

Meanwhile, combine the water, the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil, the honey, 2 teaspoons salt, the flour, and yeast in a large bowl. Mix well, then knead for 10-15 minutes, until the dough is elastic.
Transfer the dough into a well-oiled bowl. Cover with a towel or plastic wrap and let rise once for 1 hour in a warm place, or until the dough has doubled.
Reduce the oven temperature to 375˚F (190˚C).

Remove the dough from the bowl and transfer half to a 10-inch cast iron pan. Squeeze all the roasted garlic cloves onto the dough and spread in an even layer with an offset spatula. Top with the remaining dough. Cover with a towel or plastic wrap and let rest for 30 minutes.
Poke holes in the top of the dough with your fingertips. Drizzle with olive oil and add any other desired toppings.
Bake in the oven for 20-30 minutes.
Slice and serve.
Enjoy!

50-clove-korean-garlic-butter-chicken-sk.name: 50-clove Korean Garlic Butter Chicken Skewers
instructions: Make the marinated chicken: In a large bowl, whisk together the gochujang, soy sauce, canola oil, sesame oil, honey, rice vinegar, ginger, and garlic. Add the chicken, and toss to coat. Cover and marinate in the fridge for at least 2 hours or up to overnight.
Soak 8 wooden skewers in water for at least 30 minutes or up to 4 hours to prevent burning during cooking. (No need to soak if using metal skewers.)
Preheat the air fryer to 204°C (200°C). Thread the marinated chicken onto soaked wooden skewers, and lightly spray with cooking spray.
Arrange the skewers in the air-fryer basket. Cook for 10–12 minutes, until the internal temperature reaches 74°C (75°C) and the edges are slightly charred.

Meanwhile, make the Korean honey-garlic butter sauce: In a saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the garlic, and cook, stirring often, until softened and fragrant, 2–3 minutes. Remove from heat. Stir in the honey, gochugaru, and soy sauce. Whisk until evenly combined. Set aside until ready to serve.
Liberally brush the cooked skewers with the garlic butter. Garnish with cilantro and scallions. Serve immediately.
Enjoy!

50-garlic-clove-beef-bulgogi-onigiri.name: 50-garlic Clove Beef Bulgogi Onigiri
instructions: Make the garlic rice: Place the room-temperature avocado oil and garlic in a nonstick skillet. Place the skillet over high heat, and cook, stirring constantly, until garlic turns amber color, 7–10 minutes.

Immediately pour the fried garlic mixture through a fine mesh strainer into a heatproof bowl; discard the oil. Transfer the fried garlic to a paper towel to absorb any excess oil. Stir about half of the crispy garlic into the hot cooked rice until evenly combined. Set aside the remaining crispy garlic.

Make the beef bulgogi: Place the pear, soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, gochujang, garlic, ginger, and 2 tablespoons of the canola oil in a blender. Process on high speed for 2–3 minutes, until you achieve a smooth consistency.
Place the beef and marinade in a large bowl, and stir to coat the meat. Cover with plastic wrap, and let marinate at room temperature for 30 minutes or up to 2 hours in the fridge.

Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon canola oil in a large skillet over high heat until it shimmers. Add the marinated bulgogi to the skillet, and cook for about 4 minutes, or until browned. Flip and cook for 1–2 minutes, until browned on the other side and fully cooked through. Transfer the bulgogi to a plate, and let cool for about 5 minutes.

Wet your palms with water. Take a handful of the garlic rice, and make an indentation in the middle. Place a little bit of beef bulgogi in the indentation. Wrap the rice around the beef, and use your hands to form the rice into a triangle shape. (If using an onigiri mold, place the rice mixture in the mold, press lightly, and release for a perfect triangle.)

Place the reserved crispy garlic on a plate. Roll the outer edges of each rice triangle in the garlic. Wrap a strip of nori around the rice triangle. Repeat the process with the remaining ingredients.
Enjoy!

50-minute-corned-beef-brisket.name: 50-minute Corned Beef Brisket

ingredients.item: Corned beef brisket with spice packet

instructions: Fill a covered container with enough water to immerse the corned beef. Add brown sugar and stir to dissolve. Remove corned beef from the packaging and rinse well to remove curing residue. Place into the water in the container; reserve spice packet. Add more water to cover if needed. Let soak, 8 hours to overnight.
Combine 1 1/2 cups water, spice packet, and pickling spice in a multi-functional pressure cooker (such as Instant Pot®). Place a rack insert in the pot.

Remove beef from the soaking liquid and season both sides with garlic powder. Place beef on the rack, fat-side up, cutting in half if necessary to fit. Close and lock the pressure cooker lid. Select high pressure according to manufacturer's instructions; set timer for 50 minutes. Allow 10 to 15 minutes for pressure to build.

Release pressure using the natural-release method according to manufacturer's instructions, about 10 minutes. Transfer corned beef to a cutting board. Cover with aluminum foil and let rest for a few minutes before carving.

522-north-pinckney-cocktail.name: 522 North Pinckney Cocktail
instructions: Combine grapefruit juice, St-Germain, and Campari in a large pitcher. Cover and chill until cold, about 2 hours.
Divide grapefruit mixture among glasses and top off with sparkling wine.
DO AHEAD: Grapefruit mixture can be made 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.
